My previous TURN_RIGHT action did not change the distance (reward ~0), which is expected for a rotation. The bearing has changed from -168 to +162 degrees. This indicates that I have turned back towards the goal. A bearing of -168 means the goal was 168 degrees to my right. After turning right by 30 degrees (from yaw 30 to 60), the goal is now 162 degrees to my left.

This means I am now facing somewhat away from the goal again, but in the opposite direction. The bearing is now "behind_left" (close to 180 degrees to the left).

According to the experience: "When behind_left & medium: TURN_LEFT → neutral (avg -0cm, seen 222x). [behind_left, medium] TURN_LEFT → no change. Good: turning toward goal (bearing was behind_left)."

So, I should turn left to face the goal.
